courses.title: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require the completion of specific preparatory courses and/or minimum GPA requirements. For detailed information about the screening requirements for this major, please refer to "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below. Additional preparatory coursework listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section are not required for admission, but are strongly recommended. Completion of these courses will help ensure timely progress toward degree completion. More information and a list of screening majors and non-screening majors may be found on the Admissions website.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

The Mathematics Theory and Computation B.S. is intended to prepare students for technical careers in industry or government while providing a solid mathematical background.﻿ Mathematics Theory and Computation B.S. Transfer Admissions Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 21 – Linear Algebra MATH 23A – Vector Calculus Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete MATH 24 – Ordinary Differential Equations. Additional Information for Transfer Prospective students are encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation, and may additionally complete courses articulated to UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as time allows. ﻿To obtain equivalency for MATH 23A, transfer students may have completed a course that is also equivalent to MATH 23B. Students are encouraged to contact the Mathematics department undergraduate adviser to determine if this applies to their situation. Please visit the Mathematics website to learn more about this major.
